steps.title: Estimate the slope
steps.content: The data drops from near $y=90$ (small $x$) down to $y=0$ (at $x=8$) — a drop of about $90$ over a run of $8$. That gives a slope near $-10$, not $-0.1$. This eliminates choices B and C, which both have slope $-0.1$.

steps.title: Compare A and D using a clean data point
steps.content:
Both remaining choices have slope $-10$, so the deciding factor is the $y$-intercept ($80$ vs $90$).

Test at $x = 4$, where the scatterplot shows $y = 50$:
  • Choice A — $y = 80 - 10x$: $y = 80 - 40 = 40$. Misses by $10$. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 90 - 10x$: $y = 90 - 40 = 50$. Matches exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
